title: Debug a Pending pod — scheduler nodeSelector mismatch
scenario: A `gpu-workload` pod has been Pending for 10 minutes. The pod's `nodeSelector` requires `accelerator: nvidia-tesla-v100`, but neither cluster node carries that label. The cluster has two nodes with only `kubernetes.io/hostname` labels. Fix the pod so it can be scheduled.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: gpu-workload
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: gpu-workload
spec:
  nodeSelector:
    accelerator: nvidia-tesla-v100
  containers:
  - name: trainer
    image: tensorflow/tensorflow:2.14-gpu
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "500m"
        memory: "1Gi"

hints.text: Check `kubectl get events` — you'll see `FailedScheduling: 0/2 nodes available`. The nodeSelector requires a label no node has.
The cluster nodes (`sim-node-1`, `sim-node-2`) only have `kubernetes.io/hostname` labels. Remove the `nodeSelector` field entirely to let the scheduler pick any ready node.
Delete the entire `spec.nodeSelector` block and re-apply. The scheduler will assign the pod to a ready node.

solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: gpu-workload
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: gpu-workload
spec:
  containers:
  - name: trainer
    image: tensorflow/tensorflow:2.14-gpu
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "500m"
        memory: "1Gi"
